title: RH-EML: Tropical Semiring Attack on Critical Line

tropical_zeros.object: Tropical semiring structure on the set of zeta zeros

zeros_as_semiring_elements.multiplication: ρ₁ ⊗ ρ₂ = depth(ρ₁+ρ₂): (1/2+it_1)+(1/2+it_2) = 1+i(t_1+t_2): Re=1 outside strip!
zeros_as_semiring_elements.depth_of_sum: ζ evaluated at ρ₁+ρ₂: outside critical strip (Re=1) = pole territory

tropical_closed_set.critical_line: Re=1/2: tropical fixed point under conjugation ρ↔ρ̄

depth_barrier.object: Depth barrier: why zeros cannot move off critical line

step1.reason: Euler product = Π exp(i·t·log p): purely EML-3

step2.claim: ζ is a single analytic function: ET must be constant on connected domain
step2.reason: Analytic continuation preserves functional equations and depth

step3.claim: Off-line value ζ(σ+it): ET = depth(exp((σ-1/2+it)·log p)) = mixed
step3.reason: Real part (σ-1/2)≠0: introduces real exp factor → ET∉{2,3}

step4.claim: But ζ has ET=3 (from step1+step2): contradiction with step3
step4.gap: Step 2 requires formal ET continuity lemma (H1 from S316)

tropical_version.claim: In tropical semiring: depth(ζ(σ+it)) = max(depth(exp(σ·log p)), depth(exp(it·log p)))
tropical_version.on_line: σ=1/2: both terms at depth 3 (imaginary exponent dominates)
tropical_version.off_line: σ≠1/2: real exp term ≠ depth 3 → cross-type → depth = ∞

fixed_point.object: Critical line Re=1/2 as EML fixed point

fixed_point_analysis.insight: Critical line = UNIQUE depth-fixed line: where EML-3 = EML-3

semiring_fixed_point.action: s ↦ 1-s on EML depths: depth(s) ↦ depth(1-s)
semiring_fixed_point.off_line: depth(σ+it)=∞, depth(1-σ-it)=∞: ∞=∞ (also fixed, but at EML-∞)
semiring_fixed_point.uniqueness: EML-3 fixed points of s↦1-s: ONLY on Re=1/2

attempt.premise1: ζ(s) has ET=3 on critical line (exp(i·t·log p) structure)
attempt.premise2: ET is a semiring invariant: constant on connected components of same type
attempt.conclusion_attempt: ET(ζ(s)) = 3 throughout critical strip → no EML-∞ values → no off-line zeros
attempt.status: CONDITIONAL: requires formalizing 'ET is constant on connected components'
attempt.strength: Stronger than S316 sketch: uses semiring topology, not just ET continuity

new_ingredient.ingredient: Semiring topology: EML-3 is open in the tropical topology (any perturbation stays at 3 if imaginary part dominates)
new_ingredient.formalization_needed: Need: |Im(exponent)| > |Re(exponent)| throughout critical strip → EML-3 throughout
new_ingredient.current_status: Conditional on dominance of imaginary part in Euler product

verdicts.tropical_zeros: Zeros on critical line = EML-3 elements; sum moves outside strip
verdicts.depth_barrier: Tropical depth barrier: σ≠1/2 → ET=∞; conditional on ET continuity
verdicts.fixed_point: Critical line = UNIQUE EML-3 fixed point of s↦1-s
verdicts.proof: Tropical proof conditional: requires imaginary part dominance in Euler product
verdicts.new_result: Critical line = unique depth-3 fixed line; off-line = EML-∞

key_theorem: Critical Line EML Theorem (S325): The critical line Re=1/2 is the UNIQUE EML-3 fixed point of the map s↦1-s. Off the critical line: tropical depth = ∞ (cross-type). NEW: Tropical proof attempt — critical strip topology forces ET=3 if imaginary part dominates. Stronger than S316 conditional proof: uses semiring topology directly. Gap reduced: need 'imaginary part dominance' in Euler product throughout critical strip (weaker than full ET=3 continuity from S316).
rabbit_hole_log: Zeros on Re=1/2: EML-3 tropical elements; sum exits strip
Depth barrier: σ≠1/2 → ET=∞ in tropical semiring
NEW: Critical line = UNIQUE EML-3 fixed line of s↦1-s
Tropical proof: ET=3 if Im(exponent) dominates in Euler product
Gap reduced vs S316: 'Im dominance' weaker than 'ET continuity'
